metadata.summary: Native multimodal support for images, video, audio, PDFs, and Python code execution. Context caching cuts costs 75%. Vertex AI enterprise integration included.

content.title: Gemini Multimodal Integration
content.summary: Gemini is the only frontier model with native video understanding (no frame extraction), native audio processing (no separate Whisper call), and a 2M-token context window. For multi-document pipelines, context caching cuts costs 75% vs. full-price per call. Without the polling guard and file-expiry handler, agents loop indefinitely or silently re-upload duplicate files.

why.p: Gemini is the only frontier model with native video understanding (no frame extraction), native audio processing (no separate Whisper call), and a 2M-token context window. For multi-document pipelines, context caching cuts costs 75% vs. full-price per call. Without the polling guard and file-expiry handler, agents loop indefinitely or silently re-upload duplicate files.

list.item: Processing video natively — no frame extraction or separate transcription pipeline needed
Audio transcription and analysis without a separate Whisper call
Long document pipelines — 2M token context handles books, large codebases, multi-document sets
Combined modality tasks: video + PDF slides, image + audio explanation, multiple images compared
Code execution tasks where the model must compute and return results
Enterprise Google Cloud deployments requiring CMEK, VPC Service Controls, IAM (Vertex AI)
Simple text-only tasks — adds SDK complexity if already on OpenAI/Anthropic
When maximum reasoning depth matters — Claude Opus and o1 outperform Gemini on complex multi-step reasoning
Privacy-sensitive content that cannot leave on-premises — Gemini requires upload to Google servers
Low-latency real-time voice — Gemini Live API is more complex than OpenAI Realtime API
Teams with deep OpenAI or Anthropic expertise — switching SDK adds friction for marginal gains on text tasks

section.name: Modalities
section.p: Gemini supports five modalities with distinct constraints:

list.item: Images: JPG, PNG, WEBP, HEIC, HEIF — max 20MB — vision, OCR, analysis
Video: MP4, MPEG, MOV, AVI, FLV, MPG, WEBM, WMV, 3GPP — max 2GB — requires async polling
Audio: MP3, WAV, AIFF, AAC, OGG, FLAC — max 100MB — transcription, speaker ID, sentiment
PDFs: max 100MB — document Q&A, table/multi-column extraction
Code execution: Python sandbox only, no internet, ~30s execution limit

rule.statement: Load images via PIL.Image or pass raw bytes; Gemini handles HEIC/HEIF natively.
rule.rationale: Some image formats require conversion on the client side if you're not using Gemini's native support; using PIL avoids client-side preprocessing.

rule.statement: Pass multiple images in a single generate_content call to compare or analyze relationships between images.
rule.rationale: Multiple images in one call are cheaper than N separate calls; Gemini understands relationships without explicit prompting.

example.title: Single and multiple image analysis
example.code:
import PIL.Image import google.generativeai as genai
genai.configure(api_key="YOUR_API_KEY") model = genai.GenerativeModel("gemini-2.0-flash")
# Single image image = PIL.Image.open("photo.jpg") response = model.generate_content(["Describe this image in detail", image])
# Multiple images for comparison img1 = PIL.Image.open("before.jpg") img2 = PIL.Image.open("after.jpg") response = model.generate_content(["Compare these images and describe differences", img1, img2])

rule.statement: Always poll until state is ACTIVE or FAILED before passing to the model; poll interval should be 5 seconds.
rule.rationale: Audio files take time to process; polling too frequently wastes quota, polling too slowly delays the result.

example.title: Audio transcription and analysis
example.code:
import time audio_file = genai.upload_file("podcast.mp3") while audio_file.state.name == "PROCESSING": time.sleep(5) audio_file = genai.get_file(audio_file.name)
model = genai.GenerativeModel("gemini-1.5-pro") response = model.generate_content(["Transcribe this audio recording", audio_file]) # Also useful: speaker identification, sentiment, topic extraction

rule.statement: Use Gemini for PDF extraction over custom PDF libraries — it handles tables, images, and multi-column layouts natively without client-side parsing.
rule.rationale: Custom PDF parsers struggle with non-standard layouts; Gemini's vision understanding extracts content more reliably.

example.title: Structured extraction from PDF
example.code:
pdf_file = genai.upload_file("document.pdf") model = genai.GenerativeModel("gemini-1.5-pro")
# Structured extraction response = model.generate_content([ """Extract the following in JSON: {"party_names": [...], "contract_value": ..., "key_dates": [...], "payment_terms": "..."}""", pdf_file ])
example.note: Gemini handles tables, images, and multi-column layouts natively — no custom PDF parser needed.

rule.statement: Store uploaded file names with timestamps; re-upload if older than 48 hours — Gemini files expire after 48 hours.
rule.rationale: File upload is not idempotent; uploading the same file twice creates two separate file objects, doubling storage cost.

example.title: Video and PDF cross-reference
example.code: # Video + PDF cross-reference video_file = genai.upload_file("presentation.mp4") pdf_file = genai.upload_file("slides.pdf") response = model.generate_content([ "Compare the video presentation with the PDF slides. Note any discrepancies.", video_file, pdf_file ])

rule.statement: Always add a max-iteration guard to the video processing polling loop — a stuck PROCESSING state loops forever without it.
rule.rationale: Gemini video processing can hang in PROCESSING indefinitely for corrupted or unsupported files; the guard prevents infinite loops.

rule.statement: Handle FAILED state explicitly and raise an error — do not treat it as a retry condition.
rule.rationale: A FAILED state means the file cannot be processed; retrying wastes time and quota.

example.title: Async video analysis with max-iteration guard

rule.statement: Use GCS URIs (`gs://bucket/file.mp4`) instead of SDK uploads for large or frequently-accessed files — faster, no re-upload if the file already exists in GCS.
rule.rationale: SDK uploads copy the file on every pipeline run; GCS URIs reference existing cloud storage without transfer cost.

example.title: GCS URI pattern with Vertex AI
example.code:
from vertexai.generative_models import GenerativeModel, Part
video_part = Part.from_uri(uri="gs://your-bucket/video.mp4", mime_type="video/mp4") model = GenerativeModel("gemini-1.5-pro") response = model.generate_content(["Summarize this video", video_part])

example.title: Timestamp queries in video
example.code: # Extract structured info with timestamps response = model.generate_content([ """Analyze this video and extract: {"topics": [...], "key_timestamps": [{"time": "MM:SS", "event": "..."}], "action_items": [...]} Return only valid JSON.""", video_file ])
example.note: Gemini resolves timestamp references ("what happens at 2:30") natively; no frame extraction needed.

rule.statement: Check token count before creating a cache — creating a cache on content below 32K tokens raises an API error.
rule.rationale: The 32K minimum is a hard API constraint; attempting to cache small documents wastes a round-trip and surfaces an uninformative error.

rule.statement: Set TTL to match query pattern: 1 hour for interactive sessions, 24 hours for batch jobs. Extend before expiry, not after.
rule.rationale: Expired caches require re-creation including re-uploading the document; extending TTL is a cheaper operation than re-upload.

example.title: Context cache creation
example.code:
from google.generativeai import caching import google.generativeai as genai
document = genai.upload_file("large_document.pdf")
cache = caching.CachedContent.create( model="gemini-1.5-pro", display_name="Large document cache", system_instruction="You are an expert document analyzer.", contents=[document], ttl="3600s" # 1 hour ) print(f"Cache created: {cache.name}, tokens: {cache.usage_metadata.total_token_count}")

example.title: Using cached content for multi-turn Q&A
example.code:
model = genai.GenerativeModel.from_cached_content(cache)
# All queries reuse cached document tokens — 75% cheaper than full-price input response = model.generate_content("Summarize the main points") response2 = model.generate_content("What are the key dates mentioned?")
example.note: Cost: $0.01875/M tokens cached vs $0.075/M tokens uncached (gemini-1.5-pro). Break-even at 2+ queries.

example.title: Cache lifecycle management
example.code:
# List all caches for c in caching.CachedContent.list(): print(f"{c.name}: {c.display_name}")
# Extend TTL cache.update(ttl="7200s")
# Delete when done cache.delete()

rule.statement: Access executable_code and code_execution_result as separate content parts — iterate response.candidates[0].content.parts and check hasattr for each.
rule.rationale: Code and output are distinct part types; indexing blindly into content returns whichever part happens to be first.

example.title: Code execution setup
Data analysis with CSV
example.code:
model = genai.GenerativeModel( model_name="gemini-1.5-pro", tools=["code_execution"] ) csv_file = genai.upload_file("sales_data.csv")
response = model.generate_content([ "Analyze this sales data. Calculate monthly averages, find top performers, summarize trends.", csv_file ])
example.note: Sandbox has no internet access and ~30s execution limit. Results may be truncated for long-running computations.

rule.statement: Vertex AI uses Application Default Credentials (gcloud auth application-default login), not GOOGLE_API_KEY — these are not interchangeable.
rule.rationale: Mixing auth methods causes silent 403 errors; Vertex AI IAM roles are applied to the service account, not to an API key.

rule.statement: Use Vertex AI when the deployment requires CMEK, VPC isolation, or SOC2/HIPAA compliance — the Gemini Developer API does not provide these guarantees.
rule.rationale: Enterprise compliance requirements are contractual; the Developer API is designed for prototyping, not regulated data handling.

example.title: Vertex AI setup
example.code:
import vertexai from vertexai.generative_models import GenerativeModel, Part
vertexai.init(project="your-gcp-project", location="us-central1") model = GenerativeModel("gemini-1.5-pro") response = model.generate_content("Explain quantum computing")

example.title: Multimodal with GCS URIs in Vertex AI
example.code:
from vertexai.generative_models import Part
video_part = Part.from_uri(uri="gs://your-bucket/video.mp4", mime_type="video/mp4") model = GenerativeModel("gemini-1.5-pro") response = model.generate_content(["Summarize this video", video_part])

list.item: VPC Service Controls: network isolation — prevent data exfiltration
CMEK: Customer-Managed Encryption Keys for data at rest
Cloud Audit Logs: every API call logged to Cloud Logging
IAM: fine-grained access control per model and project
Model Tuning: supervised fine-tuning on Vertex AI datasets
Evaluation: built-in evaluation metrics for model quality

section.name: Agent Integration
section.p: A multimodal agent using Gemini separates file management from inference: files are uploaded once (or referenced via GCS URI), and the file reference is passed to multiple inference calls. For high-traffic document Q&A, use context caching: upload the document, create a cache, and instantiate the model from the cache so all questions reuse the cached tokens. For video analysis pipelines, implement a state machine (upload → poll → analyze) rather than blocking on the upload.

list.item: faion-sdd-executor-agent — can invoke Gemini for design document generation from uploaded architecture diagrams
A dedicated media-analysis-agent that handles file upload, polling, and extraction, exposing a simple analyze(file_path, query) interface

section.p: Multi-turn document Q&A with context caching:
section.code: model = genai.GenerativeModel.from_cached_content(cache) chat = model.start_chat() # All messages in this chat reuse cached document tokens answer = chat.send_message("What are the payment terms?")

list.item: google-generativeai — Gemini Developer API (non-Vertex). Install: pip install google-generativeai. Docs: https://ai.google.dev
vertexai — Google Cloud Vertex AI SDK (enterprise). Install: pip install vertexai. Docs: https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/docs
google-cloud-aiplatform — Lower-level Vertex AI client. Install: pip install google-cloud-aiplatform
Pillow — Image loading for PIL.Image objects. Install: pip install Pillow
gcloud (CLI) — GCS upload, IAM, project management. Docs: https://cloud.google.com/sdk/docs/install
Gemini Developer API — SaaS, free tier available, genai SDK, agent-friendly
Vertex AI (Gemini) — SaaS, enterprise with IAM auth + CMEK + VPC, agent-friendly
Google Cloud Storage — SaaS, use GCS URIs instead of uploads for large/persistent files, agent-friendly
Firebase App Check — SaaS, mobile-facing, not for server-to-server agent calls, partial agent support
Google AI Studio — SaaS, web UI for prototyping, not for production agent calls, partial agent support
Use GCS URIs (gs://bucket/file.mp4) instead of uploading via SDK for large files — faster, no re-upload if file already exists in GCS
Set TTL on context caches to match your query pattern — 1 hour for interactive sessions, 24 hours for batch jobs
Use gemini-2.0-flash for fast, cheap multimodal tasks; gemini-1.5-pro for long video and complex reasoning
Specify output format explicitly in the prompt (JSON, markdown table, bullet list) — Gemini follows format instructions reliably
For PDF extraction, prefer Gemini over custom PDF parsers — it handles tables, images, and multi-column layouts natively
Handle the 48-hour file expiry: store file.name in a DB with upload timestamp and re-upload if expired
For code execution, inspect part.executable_code and part.code_execution_result separately — they are distinct content parts

antipattern.description: File upload is not idempotent — uploading the same file twice creates two separate file objects; cache file names to avoid duplicate uploads
antipattern.reason: Re-uploading wastes storage quota and creates orphaned file objects that consume space until expiry.

antipattern.description: The polling loop must have a max-iteration guard — a stuck "PROCESSING" state will loop forever without it
antipattern.reason: Video processing can hang indefinitely for corrupted or unsupported files; without a guard, the agent stalls indefinitely.

antipattern.description: model.generate_content() is synchronous; for concurrent agent workers, use asyncio with aiohttp or the async Vertex SDK
antipattern.reason: Blocking calls prevent parallel processing; async patterns are necessary for high-throughput pipelines.

antipattern.description: Context cache requires a minimum of 32K tokens — if your document is smaller, caching raises an error; check token count before creating
antipattern.reason: Attempting to cache small documents wastes a round-trip and surfaces an API error; check first.

antipattern.description: Gemini's 2M context window tempts agents to stuff everything in one call — but cost is linear with tokens; use retrieval for large corpora
antipattern.reason: Shoving all documents into one call maximizes latency and cost; chunking + retrieval is more efficient.

antipattern.description: Code execution results may be truncated for long-running computations; the sandbox has execution time limits (~30s)
antipattern.reason: Long-running code fails silently with truncated output; break into smaller chunks or use external compute.

antipattern.description: Vertex AI auth uses Application Default Credentials (gcloud auth application-default login) — different from GOOGLE_API_KEY used by the Gemini Developer API
antipattern.reason: Mixing auth methods causes silent 403 errors; keep them separate per environment.

template.purpose: Async-safe video upload + polling + analysis function with max-iteration guard
Context cache create/use/manage pattern for repeated document queries
